title: Do not default to five bottom tab navigation
summary: AI-generated mobile navigation universally defaults to five bottom tabs with icon-plus-label, regardless of the number of primary sections.
instruction: Choose bottom tab count based on the app's primary sections. Tab count is an information architecture decision, not a design default. Five tabs that include filler sections ('Profile', 'Settings') suggest padding rather than deliberate IA.
rationale: When an app has three primary sections but displays five bottom tabs, the two extra tabs (commonly 'Profile' and 'Settings') suggest the tab count was chosen as a default rather than derived from the app's information architecture. Tab count should reflect the number of primary sections the user navigates between, not a convention.

violations: An app with only three primary sections displaying five bottom tabs (two filler: Profile, Settings).
An app with six primary sections crammed into five tabs with a 'More' overflow.
controls: An app with three primary sections and three bottom tabs.
An app with five primary sections displayed as five tabs without filler.
borderline: An app whose IA genuinely has five primary sections.

exceptions.description: Apps whose information architecture genuinely produces five primary sections. Platforms (iOS, Android) tab-bar guidance does not mandate a specific count.
examples: A messaging app with five sections
HIG does not mandate five tabs

detector.signalDescription: Detect bottom-positioned tab navigation with 5 tab items (icon + label pattern). Flag when filler tabs ('Profile', 'Settings') suggest padding.
limitations: Some apps genuinely have five primary sections.
Cannot distinguish filler tabs from legitimate navigation items without understanding the app's IA.

scenarios: An app whose IA genuinely produces five primary navigation items.
Platform conventions that encourage tab-bar navigation for certain app categories.
mitigations: Check for filler labels (Profile, Settings) in tabs 4-5 as padding signals. Accept documented IA rationale.

fixturePlan.description: A mobile app with five bottom tabs where two are filler (Profile, Settings) for a 3-section app.
Same app with three bottom tabs matching the three primary sections.
An app whose IA genuinely requires five bottom tabs.

notes: Normalization correction: removed unsupported 'three to five tabs is normal' claim. Preserved IA-follows-tab-count principle. High FP risk retained.
